original_abstract:
Deep learning has achieved remarkable success across diverse fields, from computer vision to natural language processing. However, as models continue to improve, they have grown increasingly complex, leading to massive parameter counts, higher latency, and substantial computational costs. To address these burgeoning resource demands, researchers must now look beyond mere predictive quality and focus on the overall efficiency and footprint of their models.

This survey provides a comprehensive exploration of the efficient deep learning landscape. We begin by defining the problem of efficiency and its various dimensions, followed by a rigorous examination of five core areas: compression techniques, learning algorithms, automation, efficient architectures, and infrastructure (including both software and hardware). To bridge the gap between theory and practice, we provide an experiment-backed guide and code designed to help practitioners optimize their models for real-world training and deployment. By synthesizing the current state of modeling techniques with the underlying hardware support, this work serves as an essential mental model for those looking to implement immediate efficiency gains and a foundation for further research into achieving pareto-optimal performance.
humanized_abstract:
Deep learning has achieved incredible successes in a wide range of applications, from computer vision to speech recognition to natural language processing. As our models get more accurate, they are also becoming more complex. They are often characterized by a large number of parameters, slow inference times, and high computational and memory requirements. It is simply not acceptable anymore to focus solely on the accuracy of a model. The conversation has to change to how we can develop models that are not only accurate but also efficient and lean.

This survey is a tour through the efficient deep learning landscape. We start by discussing what it means to be efficient and then dive into the five pillars of efficiency which are: - Compression - Learning - Automation - Models - Infrastructure (Software and Hardware) We provide an experimental guide and corresponding code that can serve as a practical resource for any practitioner looking to build efficient models that can be used in training and in production. We combine the art of modeling with the science of current hardware to provide a mental model that yields instant gains and a future research direction that aims to lead to the optimal performance possible.
